summary: Compared os_implementable_improvement_plan_full_memory_v1 against the current DinoBrain repository and dinobrain-data state. Identified that current DinoBrain has the OS loop/tooling skeleton but lacks several document-required closed-loop proof surfaces: reproducible full-memory freshness, review/semantic-job settlement, true answer-quality evaluation, complete provenance closure, Korean UI proof, agent parity/new-PC recovery E2E, and task lifecycle finish-gate integrity.
decisions: Treated this as a read-only audit/comparison task; no code or data mutations were made.
Used the plan document as the target standard and local repo/data evidence as the current-state baseline.
Classified gaps by severity based on whether they block the user's desired always-on, compounding, GitHub-backed OS loop.
next_steps: Start with a narrow P0 implementation pass for status freshness/full-memory audit proof so later claims have a trustworthy current baseline.
Then burn down review/semantic-job queues and add lifecycle finish-gate integrity before claiming knowledge compounding.
Add real answer-quality evaluation and visible Korean Observatory proof for every P0/P1 status before a v2 readiness claim.
search_queries: Compare os_implementable_improvement_plan_full_memory_v1 P0/P1/P2 work packages against local DinoBrain repo and dinobrain-data evidence
Inspect package scripts, OS contract, verification docs, retrieval/eval/gate implementation, and data repo backlog counts
